title: The Pretraining Milestone
concept: PHASE D2 COMPLETE. We have mastered LLM pretraining, tokenization, distributed scaling, and error recovery. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D2: Pretraining LLMs is complete. Next up: In-Context Learning.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary goal of an LLM during the pretraining phase?

options.text: Predict the next word in a sequence.
Look up answers directly on the internet.

exercises.explain: Pretraining teaches the model to predict the next word (token) in a sequence by analyzing patterns in massive datasets.

cards.title: The Peak of Pretraining
cards.body: We have climbed the biggest mountain in AI! By training our model on massive datasets using distributed systems, we've transformed random numbers into a brain that understands language. Let's celebrate completing Phase D2!

cards.title: What is the Pretraining Milestone?
cards.body: The Pretraining Milestone represents the successful completion of training a base LLM. It means we have successfully converted raw text into tokens, scaled the training process across multiple computers, and handled hardware crashes using checkpoints to create a model that understands the patterns of language.

cards.title: The Master Library
cards.body: Imagine building a massive library. Pretraining is like gathering millions of books and teaching a robot to read them all to learn how words fit together. Distributed scaling is like hiring a team of librarians to read different sections at the same time to speed things up, while error recovery is keeping a bookmark so if a librarian drops a book, they don't have to start reading from page one.

cards.title: From Random Guesses to Sentences
cards.body: Before this milestone, our model could only guess random letters. After pretraining on billions of web pages using distributed GPUs, the model can now smoothly complete sentences like 'The sky is...' with 'blue', showing it has captured the structure of human language.

cards.title: Is It Ready to Chat?
cards.body: Many people think that once a model is pretrained, it is ready to be a helpful assistant. Actually, a pretrained model is just a super-powered autocomplete! It needs further training (like fine-tuning and in-context learning) to follow instructions and hold conversations.

cards.title: Before vs. After Pretraining
cards.body: Compare what the model does before and after reaching the pretraining milestone.

right.text: Accurately predicts the next word in a sentence, acting as a powerful autocomplete.

left.text: Predicts random words and gibberish because it doesn't understand language patterns yet.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use distributed scaling when pretraining an LLM?

options.text: It allows the model to run without using any electricity.
It shares the massive training workload across many computers at the same time.
It deletes all training data to save hard drive space.
It translates the model into different human languages automatically.

exercises.explain: Distributed scaling splits the massive workload of reading and learning from billions of words across many GPUs working together at the same time.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Pretraining a modern LLM requires massive datasets, tokenization, and distributed computing power.
exercises.explain: Pretraining requires massive amounts of raw text, tokenizers to break it down, and distributed GPU clusters to handle the heavy computations.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to describe how we protect our training progress.
exercises.sentence: To prevent starting over when a server crashes, we save our progress using check_here.
exercises.explain: Checkpoints are saved copies of the model's progress. They allow us to resume training quickly if a computer crashes during the long pretraining process.

exercises.prompt: Which behavior shows a model that has just completed basic pretraining (acting as a next-token predictor) rather than chat instruction tuning?

options.text: User: 'Once upon a time, there was a' AI: 'little rabbit who lived in a cozy forest.'
User: 'Write a poem about coding.' AI: 'Sure, here is a poem: Coding is fun, lines of code under the sun...'

exercises.explain: Option B represents an AI that has completed pretraining (it acts as a raw text predictor/autocomplete). Option A is not pretrained, and Option C shows a model that has been fine-tuned to follow chat instructions, which comes after pretraining.

exercises.prompt: Looking back at Phase D2, what is the core recipe we used to create a foundation Language Model?

options.text: We teach the model specific rules and grammar guidelines before it reads anything.
We train it on raw text to predict the next word, scaling it up across computers.
We skip tokenization and feed raw pixels of text directly to the model.
We only train it on a single computer without saving any progress.

exercises.explain: We have covered the massive journey of pretraining: from chopping raw text into tokens, to training across giant clusters of GPUs, to keeping everything stable with checkpoints. Phase D2: Pretraining LLMs is complete. Next up: In-Context Learning.
